questions.question: What are the three essential properties that define a quantum channel?
questions.explanation: A quantum channel is a CPTP (completely positive, trace-preserving) linear map. Linearity ensures proper mixing of states, trace preservation keeps probabilities normalized, and complete positivity ensures the map remains physical even on subsystems.

questions.question: In the Kraus representation E(ρ) = Σₖ Kₖ ρ Kₖ†, what condition ensures trace preservation?

options.text: Kₖ Kₖ† = I for each k

questions.explanation: The trace preservation condition is Σₖ Kₖ† Kₖ = I. This ensures Tr[E(ρ)] = Tr[Σₖ Kₖ ρ Kₖ†] = Tr[ρ Σₖ Kₖ† Kₖ] = Tr[ρ] = 1.

questions.question: Which channel models T1 relaxation (energy decay to ground state)?
questions.explanation: The amplitude damping channel models T1 relaxation where the excited state |1⟩ decays to the ground state |0⟩ with some probability. This is the dominant relaxation mechanism in many physical qubit systems.

questions.question: Why is complete positivity a stronger requirement than just positivity?

options.text: It ensures the map is physical when acting on part of an entangled system

questions.explanation: Complete positivity requires that (E ⊗ I)(ρ) ≥ 0 for any extension. This ensures the channel remains physical even when acting on one part of an entangled state. The transpose map is positive but not CP, failing when applied to Bell states.

questions.question: What does the Choi matrix being positive semi-definite guarantee?

options.text: The channel has no fixed points

questions.explanation: The Choi-Jamiołkowski isomorphism establishes that a channel is completely positive if and only if its Choi matrix is positive semi-definite. This provides a convenient way to verify complete positivity.

questions.question: When composing two channels E₂(E₁(ρ)), how do the Kraus operators combine?

options.text: They commute: K = K₁ K₂ = K₂ K₁

questions.explanation: When composing channels, the Kraus operators of the composed channel are all products K₂ᵢ K₁ⱼ where i, j run over all Kraus operators. This results in m×n operators if the individual channels have m and n operators respectively.

questions.question: The phase damping channel affects the diagonal elements of the density matrix while preserving the off-diagonal elements.
questions.explanation: False. The phase damping channel does the opposite - it preserves the diagonal elements (populations) while decaying the off-diagonal elements (coherences). This models T2/Tφ decoherence where the populations are unchanged but superposition information is lost.
